behaviour.definition: The way a person acts or does things.
behaviour.example: His behaviour at school has improved a lot.

suggest.definition: To offer an idea for someone to think about.
suggest.example: I suggest we try a different way.

alternative.example: If the shop is closed, we need an alternative plan.

tactic.definition: A planned way of doing something to get a result.
tactic.example: She tried a new tactic to solve the problem.

screaming.definition: Making a loud, high sound because of strong feelings.
screaming.example: The baby was screaming because he was hungry.

cuddle.definition: To hold someone close in your arms to show love.
cuddle.example: The mother gave her child a warm cuddle.

bounce.definition: To move in a happy, energetic way.
bounce.example: The child bounced into the room with a big smile.

lonely.definition: Feeling sad because you are alone.
lonely.example: He felt lonely when his friends went home.

illustrates.definition: Shows or explains something clearly with an example.
illustrates.example: This story illustrates how kindness can help.

shift.example: Talking about your feelings can shift your mood.

acknowledge.definition: To accept or recognize that something is true.
acknowledge.example: It is important to acknowledge your child's feelings.

beneath.example: Beneath his anger, there was sadness.

tears.definition: Drops of water that come from your eyes when you cry.
tears.example: Her eyes were full of tears.

suffering.example: She did not want to see her child suffering.

upset.example: The child was upset because he lost his toy.

toddler.definition: A very young child who is learning to walk, usually between 1 and 3 years old.
toddler.example: The toddler took her first steps today.

swimming.definition: Moving through water using your arms and legs.
swimming.example: We go swimming at the pool every Saturday.

inconvenient.definition: Causing trouble or difficulty at a bad time.
inconvenient.example: The rain was inconvenient for our picnic plans.

definition.definition: The meaning of a word or the description of something.
definition.example: What is the definition of this word?

routine.definition: The usual way you do things, in the same order each time.
routine.example: Children feel safe when they have a routine.

flexibility.definition: The ability to change and adjust to new situations.
flexibility.example: Young children haven't yet learned flexibility.

articulate.definition: To express your thoughts or feelings clearly in words.
articulate.example: He found it hard to articulate what he wanted.

harassed.definition: Feeling stressed and tired because of too much pressure.
harassed.example: The harassed mother was trying to manage everything.

hurried.definition: Done quickly because there is not enough time.
hurried.example: He ate a hurried breakfast and ran out the door.

brimming.definition: Very full of something, almost overflowing.
brimming.example: Her eyes were brimming with tears.

deviate.definition: To go away from the usual or expected way.
deviate.example: If you deviate from the plan, tell everyone first.

rehearsing.definition: Practicing something before doing it for real.
rehearsing.example: They were rehearsing the play before the show.

imagining.definition: Making a picture in your mind of something that is not real or not here yet.
imagining.example: Imagining the change ahead can help a child prepare.

lift.definition: A machine in a building that carries people up and down between floors (elevator).
lift.example: We took the lift to the third floor.

stairs.definition: A set of steps that go up or down inside a building.
stairs.example: She climbed the stairs to the second floor.

cross.example: Dad was very cross when they got home.

tale.definition: A story about something that happened.
tale.example: She told me the tale of what happened at the pool.

nod.definition: To move your head up and down to say yes.
nod.example: She gave a little nod to show she agreed.

sentence.definition: A group of words that makes a complete thought.
sentence.example: He practiced saying his new sentence every morning.

parent.example: Every parent wants what is best for their child.
